title: French police suspect arson in Fontainebleau forest near Paris
description: Firefighters continue to grapple with wildfires in the forest of Fontainebleau, 70km south of Paris, which have already burned through more than 1900 hectares. Police are now considering the possibility that the fires were started deliberately. Their suspicions were raised by the fact several fires broke out in close proximity to one another. Two arrests have already been made in connection to the fires. The latest with FRANCE 24 journalist Elits

title: Watch live: France's Bastille Day parade to showcase European support for Ukraine
description: France's annual July 14 military parade will feature aircraft from 11 European countries and around 500 troops from the Coalition of the Willing allies that have pledged to help with Ukraine’s post-war security in a showcase of support for Kyiv. The event will also mark President Emmanuel Macron’s last Bastille Day in his role. Watch the parade on FRANCE 24 from 10am Paris time by clicking on our liveplayer.

title: Houthis launch missiles at Saudi Arabia, in retaliation for Sanaa airport strikes
description: Clashes have resumed in Yemen, with Houthis rebels blaming Saudi Arabia and the country's internationally recognised government for strikes on Sanaa Airport. The hits were designed to prevent an Iranian plane, carrying a Houthis delegation, from landing. Story by Ellen Gainsford.

title: US strikes in Iran continue, Trump says reinstates blockade on Strait of Hormuz
description: US President Donald Trump says he is reinstating a blockade on Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and will charge ships for safe passage. The announcement comes after the two countries exchanged more fire following an Iranian attack on a ship in the waterway on Sunday. The UN's shipping agency says they have consistently opposed any nation charging a fee for passage through straits used ​for international navigation. Details by Morgan Ayre.

title: Bastille Day: France celebrates Ukraine and its allies with military parade
description: France celebrates its national holiday today, celebrating the taking of the Bastille in 1789 that kickstarted the French Revolution. As ever, it is being marked by a massive military parade. title: Gulf, Arab states welcome US-Iran deal to end war, reopen Hormuz
description:
DUBAI: Gulf and Arab states welcomed on Monday a US-Iran agreement to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, expressing hope that the deal will pave the way for lasting regional stability and broader diplomatic engagement.

	Saudi Arabia welcomed the agreement between Washington and Tehran to halt military operations and begin detailed negotiations over a 60-day period aimed at reaching a permanent settlement.

SINGAPORE: Oil prices ​slipped to a three-month low on Monday after US President Donald Trump and Iran's deputy foreign minister said ‌they had reached an initial deal to end the war and to resume traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

Our colleagues at France 2 were able to accompany the pilots during rehearsals between Chartres and the French capital.

title: Sen. Lindsey Graham died of aorta tear, medical examiner says in preliminary finding
description:
Condition, called aortic dissection, tied to hardening of US lawmaker's arteries; official cause of death to be published after further testing
The post Sen. Lindsey Graham died of aorta tear, medical examiner says in preliminary finding appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Police grill Shas MK for 2nd time on suspicion of funneling taxpayer funds to party paper
description:
Case of Haim Biton, who allegedly used public funds to pay employees’ salaries while heading Shas school network, will be sent soon to prosecutors to decide on possible indictment
The post Police grill Shas MK for 2nd time on suspicion of funneling taxpayer funds to party paper appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Police arrest dozens as undercover agent busts suspected arms, drug smuggling network
description:
Police say operation marks first time Bedouin agent used for elite undercover probe; police say 4 M16s, dozens of handguns and cocaine among the contraband seized in multiple raids
The post Police arrest dozens as undercover agent busts suspected arms, drug smuggling network appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Israel’s Election Season: The opening shot - Sponsored Content
description:
What do 4 political earthquakes in a single week – as the Knesset is on the brink of dissolving – reveal about the election that will define Israel’s future?
The post Israel’s Election Season: The opening shot appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Secondhand smoke can disrupt children’s sleep in surprising ways, Israeli researchers say
description:
Scientists find 67% spike in sleep disruptions in kids aged 1-12 even without respiratory issues, suggesting nicotine may have an impact on the central nervous system
The post Secondhand smoke can disrupt childrens sleep in surprising ways, Israeli researchers say appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Coalition ploughs ahead with Karhi’s media bill, dropping entire sections last-minute
description:
Rushed committee approval comes after parts related to government app that would broadcast sports free of charge removed due to Haredi objections; High Court to discuss contentious bill Monday
The post Coalition ploughs ahead with Karhis media bill, dropping entire sections last-minute appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: USS Ford’s Super Hornet Spotted with New Mission Markings
description: The ⁠USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) aircraft carrier and Carrier Air Wing 8 recently returned from the Middle East, with its Super Hornets showing new markings from the operations against Iran. The F/A-18E/F Super Hornets of the U.S. Navy’s Carrier Air Wing (CVW) 8 were among the aircraft which took part in the flyovers for []

title: Helsing raises $1.8 billion in Europe’s biggest defense-startup round
description: Defense technology has been one of the fastest-growing areas of venture capital funding on the back of rising military budgets, and in Europe especially.

title: Europe’s Hydis project settles on concept for hypersonic interceptor
description: Europe's push to field a hypersonic-defense interceptor will come down to two competing projects.

title: Ukraine’s newest strike weapon drifts into Russia on the wind
description: The U.S. and Russia are fielding the same balloons used by Ukraine that bait air defenses and now launch missiles.

title: Senate eyes Hegseth travel cuts without probes into Iran school bombing, boat strikes
description: Senate lawmakers added a provision in the NDAA that would restrict Hegseth's travel expenses until the department releases certain operational information.

title: Ukraine hits Moscow refinery in major drone attack on Russian capital
description: Ukrainian drones hit the Russian capital’s oil refinery for the second time this week.

title: US approves Kuwait request to buy nearly $2 billion of counter-drone platforms
description: Following recent Iranian strikes on Kuwait, the U.S. approved a potential foreign military sale of counter-UAS platforms made by Anduril.

title: US strikes Iranian sites after Iran launches drones, in latest Gulf flare-up
description: U.S. forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites on Saturday after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. military said.

title: Iran: Rising regional power or too weak to make peace?
description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, July 13: Papers analyse Iran's strategy against the United States. In Japan, a quiet refuge has emerged for Russian spies. And finally, the story of a dog and its "emotional support duck"...

title: Ukraine to get new prime minister amid major government reshuffle
description: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelensky has announced a new political strategy and a government reshuffle, with Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko stepping down from her position. Zelensky says he has offered her a new role focused on relations with a key international partner, while calling for a “renewal of the Cabinet of Ministers”.  The Ukrainian leader also announced planned changes among the heads of law enforcement agencies.  No specific reaso

title: 'Coalition of the willing' meets in Paris to bolster war support to Ukraine
description: Ukrainian drone strikes killed four people and wounded seven in Russia on July 13, most in the Moscow region, as Kyiv upped its strikes in response to Kremlin's barrages. This comes as the 'coalition of the willing', a coalition of Ukraine's allies, gather in Paris. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg tells us more.

title: How heatwaves are changing France's real-estate market
description: Crude oil prices surge as renewed US-Iran strikes fuel market fears. In France, real-estate agents say property buyers are changing priorities as heatwaves become more frequent. And Cuba suffers another nationwide blackout amid the impact of US sanctions.

title: Blaze burns through 'lungs of greater Paris' Fontainebleau forest
description: Around 400 firefighters descended on Fontainebleau on Sunday as a fire broke out in the Ile de France forest. It comes just ahead of the July 14 national holiday and on the first major weekend for departures for the summer holiday season, leaving train networks disrupted and forcing highways to close in the Paris region.

title: Strait of Hormuz 'back to square one' as US-Iran flare-up brings uncertainty
description: The renewed hostilities in the Middle East followed a renewed exchange of fire. It comes as negotiators struggle to reach a lasting peace deal to keep the crucial Strait of Hormuz open. Although the Strait is not being completely shut down, Iran has been striking ships adopting a US-defined route through it. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani explains.

title: US-Iran talks to end war on the line amid renewed strikes
description: The US has launched more strikes in Iran, for a third major attack between July 12 and July 13 in the Middle East. It comes just hours after the US launched some of its largest air strikes against Iran since US President Donald Trump declared the ceasefire over. Iran announced it had started its retaliatory response, targeting US military bases in Jordan Bahrain and Kuwait.

title: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
description: It was the first time firefighting planes had been sent up from the normally drier and hotter south of the country to tackle fires in the Paris region.

title: Heavy rains and flash floods in Bangladesh leave 51 dead
description: Thousands have lost their homes as flash floods and landslides have hit large parts of the country.

title: EU countries consider sanctions on trade from illegal Israeli settlements
description: Pressure from EU member states to impose sanctions has increased in recent months amid escalating settler violence.
